partners-for-literacy.description: Free adult literacy, ESL, and digital literacy programs across the Cariboo Chilcotin

community-futures.description: Nonprofit with volunteer board supporting rural entrepreneurs and community economic development

wl-community-living.name: Williams Lake Association for Community Living

cara.name: Cariboo Agricultural Research Alliance
cara.description: Applied agricultural research for Cariboo farmers and ranchers

central-chilcotin-rehab.name: Central Chilcotin Rehabilitation Ltd.
central-chilcotin-rehab.description: Indigenous joint venture doing award-winning forest rehabilitation and biomass work across Tŝideldel, Tl'etinqox, and Yunesit'in territories

stampede-association.name: Williams Lake Stampede Association
stampede-association.description: Nonprofit that runs the world-famous Williams Lake Stampede on Canada Day weekend

ccacs.name: Central Cariboo Arts & Culture Society
ccacs.description: Manages the Arts Centre, grants programs, and Performances in the Park summer concert series

station-house-gallery.name: Station House Gallery
station-house-gallery.description: Williams Lake's primary visual arts gallery showcasing regional artists

cariboo-museum.name: Museum of the Cariboo Chilcotin
cariboo-museum.description: Regional history museum and home of the BC Cowboy Hall of Fame

denisiqi.name: Denisiqi Services Society
denisiqi.description: Over 20 years of culturally grounded child and family services for Tŝilhqot'in and Ulkatcho communities

cariboo-friendship.name: Cariboo Friendship Society
cariboo-friendship.description: Urban Indigenous nonprofit since 1969 providing housing, youth programs, crisis services, and The Hearth restaurant

ccatec.name: Carrier Chilcotin Aboriginal Training & Employment Centre
ccatec.description: Workforce development and employment training across Carrier, Chilcotin, and Secwépemc nations

wl-hospice.description: 100% volunteer hospice providing companionship, respite, and grief support — all donations stay in community

cmha-cariboo.name: CMHA Cariboo Chilcotin
cmha-cariboo.description: Mental health counselling, housing support, victim services, and crisis intervention in Williams Lake and 100 Mile House

ccsar.name: Central Cariboo Search and Rescue
ccsar.description: Highly trained volunteer first responders serving the central Cariboo region locally, regionally, and provincially

cmh-foundation.name: Cariboo Memorial Hospital Foundation
cmh-foundation.description: Volunteer-driven foundation that has raised nearly $4 million for hospital equipment in Williams Lake

salvation-army-wl.description: Food bank, emergency shelter, free meals, and Christmas hamper program for Williams Lake families

spca-wl.description: Animal welfare, shelter, and adoption services in Williams Lake

wl-indian-band.name: Williams Lake Indian Band
wl-indian-band.description: T'exelcemc (Williams Lake Indian Band) — self-governing First Nation with programs in language revitalization, housing, and land stewardship

sd27.name: School District 27 (Cariboo-Chilcotin)
sd27.description: Public school district serving Williams Lake, 100 Mile House, and surrounding Cariboo communities with innovative rural education programs

cariboo-regional-library.description: Public library system serving the Cariboo region with branches in Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House and more — free internet access, digital literacy programs, and community gathering spaces

boys-girls-club-wl.description: After-school programs, summer camps, and youth mentorship serving Williams Lake families since 1973

habitat-for-humanity-cariboo.description: Building affordable homes in the Cariboo and operating the ReStore — diverting building materials from landfill while funding housing

wl-food-policy-council.description: Community organization supporting local food security through community gardens, food recovery, and farm-to-table initiatives in the Cariboo
